ops.title: RESOLVED 2026-07-21 — codex quota ping succeeded well before the advertised Jul 27 reset; always ping-check, never trust a remembered block; codex-lane hygiene rules still stand

ops.body:
Codex hit a hard usage limit on 2026-07-20 ("try again at Jul 27th, 2026 10:03 AM") and sol lanes were rerouted to opus meanwhile. On 2026-07-21 a trivial ping (`codex exec "Reply with exactly: ok"`) succeeded — the block lifted days early (rolling window or account rotation, not the advertised reset). Later that same day the quota died again mid-session: a ping and one full review lane succeeded (~00:40–03:00), then the next dispatch (~05:30) hard-blocked ("try again at Jul 27th, 2026 10:12 PM"). The window is rolling and tight — a passed ping buys one dispatch, not a session; plan the opus/fable fallback into every sol-routed phase up front.

**Why:** advertised codex reset times are unreliable in both directions — a remembered block can strand sol-lane routing on opus long after quota returns, and a "fine yesterday" quota can die mid-fan-out.

**How to apply:** ping-check codex with a trivial exec immediately before any sol dispatch or reroute decision; route on the ping result, never on memory or the advertised reset time. The codex-lane hygiene lessons stand regardless of quota state: phrase review/security lanes neutrally ("verify for concrete defects", not "attack"/"break"); give wrapper lanes an explicit report-lane-failed-rather-than-fabricate instruction; after two hard misses escalate to fable, not a third rephrase.
